GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "The one change that transformed this Sydney artist's skin", "Sydney-based painter Hannah Carrick's vibrant canvases each tell a colourful story, but her beauty routine is decidedly simpler. The artist grew up in Byron Bay, so has always taken a holistic approach to aesthetics and wellness—the kind synonymous with small town living and all-natural vibes. She eats clean and mostly plant-based, and seeks out the sun and sea for "soul healing" when needed—a break from creating the bold acrylic-on-canvas pieces for which she's famed. At 32, Carrick's been tackling some old acne scarring lately and is starting to see pigmentation spots pop up. She also suffers bouts of eczema, triggered often by food and alcohol, which cause her skin to become dry and patchy. "I'm starting to see the signs of age, and just feeling like my skin doesn't look as youthful," she explained. So Vogue set this talented creative a task: to trial Vida Glow's Marine Collagen Powder for six weeks, so we could see exactly what it would do. Replenishing collagen levels via a supplement can slow the ageing process in the skin, plump and revive flagging firmness, smooth out fine lines and wrinkles and boost hydration. It also gives strength and shine to hair and nails. Intrigued by the legion of passionate fans, and the reams of clinical data around its efficacy, Carrick took to the new regimen of two sachets daily with ease. She first experimented with flavours, putting Vida Glow Blueberry Marine Collagen Powder into her morning smoothie (a banana, blueberry, oats, almond butter, coconut yoghurt, muesli and collagen combo). She eventually settled on the Original formula, twice a day—first in an organic instant coffee in the morning, and then in her peppermint tea at night. "There really is zero taste to it!" she exclaimed. Carrick, who also trained as a graphic designer before she took to painting full-time, first noticed a change in the skin of her hardworking hands. "My hands are usually pretty rough, because I'm using them, and washing them, all of the time. They were softer almost immediately," she said, of the effect of regular intake of Vida Glow Marine Collagen Powder. Hannah also noticed the texture and condition of the skin on her face changed. "That softness has been the biggest physical difference. My face feels plump and smooth, and it looks dewier," she said by day 40 of her challenge. Eczema flare ups disappeared while on her regimen of marine collagen, which was a surprising by-product to what she thought would be an entirely superficial undertaking. "Usually, I'm triggered often, and will wake up with skin that's irritated and itchy, but that hasn't happened in weeks," she explained. As for longer term benefits, Carrick has her eye on another advantage of regular doses of Vida Glow Marine Collagen—thicker, healthier hair. "It hasn't been long, but I do feel like there's more of it," she said, running her fingers through her blonde waves. And those working hands usually covered in a rainbow of paint? They now feature shinier tips. "I've always had strong nails," she said. "But I'm seeing more shine in the nails already." Shop Vida Glow's Marine Collagen Powder here."
